retry.notes: keep the nose as an outlined region (not solid ink) and keep the rump patch an outlined fillable region exactly as the pen draws it — solid chalk ink renders as glowing white in dark mode

chalk.review: The shipped 2026-07 chalk solidified the pen's solid-ink nose AND the pen's merely-outlined rump patch, so both glow solid white in dark mode (the night fill painted the patch maroon — the chalk's white covers it), and the wide white scleras read manic. Judge on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE (ISSUES Tier 1 #3); after a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — audit-fill-eyes reports night ok on the defective shipped take; the ISSUES #8 chalk-whitening class escaping the eye.

light.review: A flat-eye flag here is the known side-profile detector false positive — verified lively by eye in the 3.1 wave (gemini-3.1-migration.md). judgeLightEyes has no suppressions (ISSUES), so adjudicate visually before burning retries.
light.why: The night judge's IDEAS #12 suppressions cleared this page's night-side flag; the light judge still re-derives cores and misreads the side-profile eye.

retry.notes: the open bill interior must stay toothless — the pen has a clean open bill with a tongue outline and no teeth; do not draw teeth

chalk.review: The shipped 2026-07 chalk INVENTED a row of human-like teeth inside the open bill (the pen and the light fill have none) and the night fill colored them in — dark mode grins with teeth light mode doesn't have. Check the bill interior on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE (ISSUES Tier 1 #2). 3.1 resists erase edits on solid ink — budget extra attempts/notes; after a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — no gate sees content invention inside the subject (the invented-shapes audit only scans the open background).
